title: Article
body:
A standalone piece of writing: our default document type for essays, data
explainers and topic deep-dives. Published under `/{slug}`.

## When to use

- Any self-contained narrative piece that is not the entry page of a topic.

## When NOT to use

- Prefer `data-insight` for a short, chart-led observation.
- Prefer `topic-page` / `linear-topic-page` for the entry page of a topic.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The document body: a sequence of ArchieML component blocks. See the components reference for every available block.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The document type; decides the layout and where the document appears on the site.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The document headline.

fields.name: supertitle
fields.description: Kicker line shown above the title.

fields.name: subtitle
fields.description: Standfirst shown below the title.

fields.name: authors
fields.description: Comma-separated author names. Append a role in parentheses to credit a specific contribution — e.g. "Jane Doe (Editor), John Smith".

fields.name: dateline
fields.description: The publication date as displayed under the byline, e.g. "June 30, 2026".

fields.name: excerpt
fields.description: Short plain-text summary used in cards and link previews.

fields.name: refs
fields.description: Footnote definitions for ID-based references, authored as a `[.refs]` block of `id:` + `[.+content]` entries. Inline `{ref}…{/ref}` footnotes need no entry here.

fields.name: deprecation-notice
fields.description: One or more paragraphs shown in a banner marking the article as outdated, authored as a `[+deprecation-notice]` freeform block of text paragraphs.

fields.name: latest-feed-featured-image
fields.description: Image filename that overrides `featured-image` on the /latest feed.

fields.name: latest-feed-excerpt
fields.description: Rich-text excerpt (formatting and internal links allowed) shown on the /latest feed instead of `excerpt`, authored as a `[+latest-feed-excerpt]` freeform block of text paragraphs.

fields.name: hide-citation
fields.description: Set to true to hide the "Cite this work" section at the end of the article.

fields.name: cover-image
fields.description: Filename of the full-width image shown behind the header.

fields.name: featured-image
fields.description: Filename of the image used in cards and social-media previews.

fields.name: atom-title
fields.description: Title override for the document's atom-feed entry.

fields.name: atom-excerpt
fields.description: Excerpt override for the document's atom-feed entry.

fields.name: sidebar-toc
fields.description: Set to true to show a table of contents in the sidebar.

fields.name: heading-variant
fields.description: Typography variant for the header: "heavy" or "light".

fields.name: hide-subscribe-banner
fields.description: Set to true to hide the subscribe banner.

fields.name: cover-color
fields.description: Named background color of the header area (one of the sdg-color-N values or "amber").

fields.name: sticky-nav
fields.description: Navigation items pinned below the header, authored as a `[.sticky-nav]` array of `target:` (anchor) + `text:` (label) pairs. Linear topic pages get a default set generated automatically.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Details-on-demand dictionary sourced from the details fragment. Not yet supported by the ArchieML write-back: documents using it must be edited in Google Docs directly.

fields.name: faqs
fields.description: FAQ content consumed by data pages, authored in FAQ fragments. Not yet supported by the ArchieML write-back: documents using it must be edited in Google Docs directly.

skeleton.name: Opening
skeleton.description: One to three plain paragraphs stating the key message — no heading, no chart. The first paragraph doubles as what readers see in previews.

skeleton.name: Sections
skeleton.description: Each section opens with a level-1 heading and mixes prose with the evidence for it — usually a chart every few paragraphs. Callouts flag caveats or data notes; sticky-right pairs a discussion with a chart that stays on screen.

skeleton.name: Endnotes
skeleton.description: Optional closing matter — links to related work (prominent-link or recirc) and acknowledgements, often set off by a horizontal rule.

title: Topic Page
body:
The entry page for a topic: a modular page that opens with a
`{.topic-page-intro}` block and typically composes key insights, featured
metrics and an `{.all-charts}` section. Published under `/{slug}`.

## When to use

- The canonical landing page for a topic in the site navigation.

## When NOT to use

- Prefer `linear-topic-page` when the topic reads as one continuous essay.
- Prefer `article` for standalone pieces within a topic.

skeleton.name: Intro
skeleton.description: A topic-page-intro block — a few paragraphs on why the topic matters, with related topics and download links alongside. Every published topic page opens with one.

skeleton.name: Key insights
skeleton.description: A key-insights slider — one slide per takeaway, each pairing a heading with a chart and a short discussion.

skeleton.name: Research & writing
skeleton.description: The research-and-writing grid collecting our articles on the topic.

skeleton.name: All charts
skeleton.description: The all-charts block — the complete gallery of the topic's interactive charts, generated from the page's topic tag. Nearly every published topic page ends with it.

title: Linear Topic Page
body:
A topic entry page that reads as one continuous essay rather than a modular
grid. Gets a sticky navigation generated from its headings (override with
`sticky-nav`). Published under `/{slug}`.

## When to use

- A topic whose content is best presented as a single narrative flow.

## When NOT to use

- Prefer `topic-page` for modular topic pages composed of self-contained
  sections.

skeleton.name: Opening
skeleton.description: A short untitled introduction, often followed by featured-metrics or an ltp-toc when the page is long.

skeleton.name: Sections
skeleton.description: Level-1 headings drive the page's sticky navigation; each section reads as essay prose with the topic's charts woven in.

skeleton.name: Closing
skeleton.description: Optional endmatter — an explore-data-section pointing at the topic's explorer, related reading via recirc or prominent links.

title: Fragment
body:
Reusable content that is not published as a standalone page: fragments hold
shared material other documents pull in — most commonly the details-on-demand
dictionary and the FAQ content used by data pages.

## When to use

- Content referenced from other documents or systems rather than read in
  place.

## Limitations

- The `details` and `faqs` front-matter structures that most existing
  fragments are built around are not yet supported by the ArchieML
  write-back — those fragments must be edited in Google Docs directly. The
  write API refuses such edits rather than losing the content.

skeleton.name: The shared content
skeleton.description: Freeform body — prose and headings shaped by whatever the consuming documents need. Fragments have no canonical structure of their own.

title: Data Insight
body:
A short, chart-led observation: a single static image or chart and a few
paragraphs making one point. Published in the data-insights feed under
`/data-insights/{slug}`.

## When to use

- One interesting pattern in the data, explained in a few hundred words.

## When NOT to use

- Prefer `article` when the piece needs sections, multiple charts, or a
  narrative arc.

## Variations

- Set `grapher-url` when the insight is based on a live grapher chart,
  `narrative-chart` when it is based on a narrative chart, and `figma-url`
  to point at the Figma file for the static image.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The insight headline.

fields.name: authors
fields.description: Comma-separated author names. Append a role in parentheses to credit a specific contribution — e.g. "Jane Doe (Editor)".

fields.name: narrative-chart
fields.description: Name of the narrative chart the insight is based on.

fields.name: grapher-url
fields.description: URL of the grapher chart the insight is based on.

fields.name: figma-url
fields.description: URL of the Figma file holding the insight's static image.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The insight body: typically one image and a handful of short text paragraphs.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always "data-insight".

skeleton.name: The image
skeleton.description: One static chart image — the insight is the chart. The title above it states the finding, not the topic.

skeleton.name: The point
skeleton.description: Two to four short paragraphs explaining the single pattern the chart shows. Nearly every published data insight is exactly this — image plus prose, nothing else.

skeleton.name: Call to action
skeleton.description: Optional closing cta linking to the interactive chart or the related topic page.
